title: DSP-7878: Streamline OTel -> Prometheus OTLP metrics path (minimal infra blast radius)
description:
## Intent
This MR hardens the OTel MVP to reliably deploy in our airgapped/CD environments while keeping scope tightly focused on the telemetry pipeline. Metrics are exported via OTLP to an existing in-cluster Prometheus endpoint; backend observability tooling itself is managed outside this repo.

## Why this update
- Align behavior with working component patterns (notably `databricks-mdm` and `ng-infrastructure`) for CI/CD and deploy scripts
- Remove flaky runtime dependencies (Terraform/Helm internet fetches in airflow deploys)
- Reduce infra blast radius by removing legacy in-repo backend components that are out of MVP scope
- Clarify architecture and ownership boundaries in docs

## What changed
### 1) OTel metrics path + collector hardening
- Kept OTLP-native metrics path to Prometheus (`/api/v1/otlp/v1/metrics`)
- Hardened collector normalization/cardinality controls and semconv alignment for Prometheus OTLP flow
- Updated demo/collector values and templates to match the minimal MVP telemetry path

### 2) Airgapped deploy reliability
- Terraform deploy scripts now use explicit pinned version behavior without runtime install (`tfenv use` if present; require `terraform` in PATH)
- Deploy/destroy scripts aligned closer to known-good component patterns
- Helm deploy path for airflow uses cached internal chart flow and desired-state rewrite for cached repos
- Replaced non-portable `rg` usage in deploy shell scripts with portable `grep`

### 3) CI/CD pipeline cleanup
- Added Helm cache template integration and `push:helm-cache` configuration for OTel desired state
- Removed custom `deploy-platform` rule divergence to match template behavior used by sibling components
- Added repo-owned `make-gitlab-tag` + `tag:version` override for consistent semver/RC tag creation
- Corrected semver/tagging setup for this MVP line (`0.1.x`) and cleaned malformed historical tag

### 4) Scope reduction to minimal MVP
- Removed legacy in-repo monitoring/backend stack artifacts (Grafana/Loki/Tempo/Prometheus chart assets and monitoring layer files)
- Kept this repo focused on OTel pipeline components (operator/collectors/demo path) rather than owning backend tooling

### 5) Documentation realignment
- Updated architecture/HLD/tech-spec docs to describe backend ownership boundary clearly
- Clarified MVP statement: metrics go to an existing in-cluster Prometheus instance, but Prometheus lifecycle/config outside this repo
- Added/updated minimal operating mode and healthcheck guidance

## Validation performed
- Full branch diff reviewed against `master`
- Shell/lint issues fixed for updated scripts (including SC2086 and command portability)
- Deploy flow and CI config aligned with existing proven patterns from sibling repos

## Blast radius / compatibility
- Preserves component-centric OTel deployment model
- Intentionally removes in-repo backend observability stack ownership
- Improves deployment reliability in airflow/airgapped execution contexts

@@ -0,0 +1,146 @@
+# OTel Minimal MVP Operating Mode
+
+## Scope
+
+- OTel Operator + two collectors (`otel-infra-agent`, `otel-cluster-agent`).
+- Metrics path is active: OTel scrape/enrich/normalize -> existing in-cluster Prometheus OTLP ingest endpoint.
+- Traces/logs pipelines are present with placeholder `debug` exporters.
+- No Grafana/Loki/Tempo stack deployment in this phase.
+- No CloudWatch dashboards or demo data infrastructure in this phase.
+
+## Security and Compliance Gate
+
+- PHI/PII must never be logged by collector or application instrumentation.
+- Any attribute with patient/member identifiers must be dropped or redacted before export.
+- Secrets and endpoints must come from runtime env/SSM substitutions; no plaintext credentials in chart values.
+- IRSA is limited to collector service account identity; no wildcard AWS permissions are required in this phase.
+- RBAC is read-only and scoped to discovery/metadata enrichment needs.
+
+## Metadata Contract (MVP Required)
+
+The collector configuration must stamp or derive these attributes for managed workloads:
+
+- `service.name`
+- `deployment.component`
+- `seiji.package.version`
+- `vcs.ref.head.revision`
+- `cicd.pipeline.run.id`
+- `cloud.account.id`
+- `cloud.region`
+- `k8s.cluster.name`
+- `k8s.namespace.name`
+
+## Canonical Semconv Schema (MVP: Existing Prometheus, Long-Term: Backend-Agnostic)
+
+Canonical metric families (OpenTelemetry semconv rendered for Prometheus):
+
+- `k8s_pod_cpu_usage`
+- `k8s_pod_cpu_time_seconds_total`
+- `k8s_node_cpu_usage`
+- `k8s_node_cpu_time_seconds_total`
+- `kube_pod_container_resource_requests`
+- `kube_node_status_allocatable`
+
+Canonical query attributes:
+
+- `k8s_namespace_name`
+- `k8s_pod_name`
+- `k8s_node_name`
+- `k8s_cluster_name`
+- `service_name`
+- `metric_source=otel_normalized`
+
+Compatibility note:
+
+- Resource identity remains in OTel resource attributes and is promoted by Prometheus OTLP ingest for MVP.
+- Compatibility aliases are derived from existing metric labels only (no resource-to-metric duplication).
+
+Compatibility aliases (temporary migration window):
+
+- `namespace` <- `k8s_namespace_name`
+- `pod` <- `k8s_pod_name`
+- `node` <- `k8s_node_name`
+
+These aliases are emitted to keep existing PromQL and dashboards functional while
+teams migrate to canonical semconv labels.
+
+## Developer Interface
+
+Application teams only need OTLP SDK configuration:
+
+- Endpoint: `otel-cluster-agent-collector.otel.svc.cluster.local:4317`
+- Protocol: OTLP gRPC (or OTLP HTTP where required)
+- Required resource attributes:
+  - `service.name`
+  - `deployment.component`
+  - team ownership tag (`team.name` recommended)
+
+Custom metric rules:
+
+- Use stable names and units.
+- Avoid high-cardinality dimensions (request IDs, user IDs, UUIDs).
+- Keep labels bounded and enumerable.
+
+## Anti-Loop Guardrails
+
+- OTel-owned scrape jobs must be explicitly allowlisted.
+- Prometheus-native jobs and OTel-owned jobs are tracked separately during migration.
+- OTel writes normalized metrics with source markers (`metric_source=otel_normalized`) to prevent recursive ingestion patterns.
+
+## OTel-First Migration Guidance
+
+- Keep OTel receivers (`otlp`, selected Prometheus scrape jobs) as the ingestion edge.
+- Prefer SDK OTLP emission for application metrics as the primary path.
+- Treat Prometheus-style scrape in collectors as transitional for workloads not yet OTLP-instrumented.
+- Retire scrape jobs incrementally only after equivalent OTLP signal coverage is verified.
+
+## Semconv Rollout Phases
+
+Phase 1 (compatibility on):
+

+#!/bin/bash
+set -euE -o pipefail
+
+: "${LABEL:?LABEL must be set}"
+KUBECONFIG="/tmp/${LABEL}.kubeconfig"
+
+echo "Checking OTel operator readiness..."
+kubectl --kubeconfig "${KUBECONFIG}" -n otel wait \
+  --for=condition=available deployment/otel-operator-opentelemetry-operator --timeout=120s
+
+echo "Checking collector pods..."
+kubectl --kubeconfig "${KUBECONFIG}" -n otel get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=otel-collectors
+
+echo "Checking cluster collector CR status..."
+kubectl --kubeconfig "${KUBECONFIG}" -n otel get opentelemetrycollector otel-cluster-agent -o yaml >/dev/null
+
+echo "Checking daemonset collector CR status..."
+kubectl --kubeconfig "${KUBECONFIG}" -n otel get opentelemetrycollector otel-infra-agent -o yaml >/dev/null
+
+echo "Checking Prometheus OTLP ingest target availability..."
+kubectl --kubeconfig "${KUBECONFIG}" -n prometheus get svc prometheus-server >/dev/null
+
+echo "Checking collector OTLP exporter health (recent logs)..."
+if kubectl --kubeconfig "${KUBECONFIG}" -n otel logs deploy/otel-cluster-agent-collector --since=10m 2>/dev/null \
+  | rg -q 'status_code": 404|Exporting failed\. Dropping data\.'; then
+  echo "Collector exporter health check failed (recent export errors detected)."
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "Minimal OTel pipeline verification completed."

+import sys
+
+import boto3
+import yaml
+
+aws_session = boto3.session.Session()
+REGION = aws_session.region_name
+
+
+def update_desired_state(file_path, new_version):
+    with open(file_path) as file:
+        data = yaml.safe_load(file)
+
+    for app_name, app_details in data.get("apps", {}).items():
+        chart = app_details.get("chart", "")
+        if not any(repo in chart for repo in ["abacus/", "local-charts/"]):
+            chart_name = chart.split("/")[-1]
+            data["apps"][app_name]["chart"] = f"abacus/{chart_name}"
+            data["apps"][app_name]["version"] = new_version
+
+    if "helmRepos" in data:
+        if REGION != "us-east-1":
+            abacus_repo = f"s3://abacus-artifacts-kitchen-{REGION}/helm"
+        else:
+            abacus_repo = "s3://abacus-artifacts-kitchen/helm"
+        data["helmRepos"] = {"abacus": abacus_repo}
+
+    with open(file_path, "w") as file:
+        yaml.dump(data, file, default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    if len(sys.argv) != 3:
+        print("Usage: python dsf_helm_cache.py <desired-state-file-path> <new-version>")
+        sys.exit(1)
+
+    desired_state_file = sys.argv[1]
+    version = sys.argv[2]
+    update_desired_state(desired_state_file, version)

+#!/bin/bash
+set -euxo pipefail
+
+# this script creates either a new semver or calver tag, using the branch name, tag prefix, MR
+# labels, and the most recent tag (determined numerically, not when it was created) as inputs.
+#
+# NOTE:
+# - This repo previously produced a malformed 4-part tag (e.g. 0.0.1.0).
+# - We intentionally ignore malformed release tags when computing the next suffix.
+
+# Takes in an exact tag, e.g. 1.2.3
+function make_tag() {
+  if [ "${MAKE_TAGS:-}" != "" ]; then
+    git tag -a "$1" -m "tag created from the kitchen by gitlabhelpers.tagmaker"
+    git push origin "$1"
+  else
+    echo "New tag is $1"
+  fi
+}
+
+# Takes in a major/minor prefix, e.g. 1.1 for 1.1.x
+function make_tag_from_prefix() {
+  git fetch --tags
+
+  # find the highest-numbered release suffix matching the prefix.
+  # only accept well-formed release tags: <prefix>.<integer>, ignoring malformed tags like 0.0.1.0
+  local current_release_suffix
+  current_release_suffix=$(git tag | grep -E "^$1\\.[0-9]+$" | grep -v 'rc' | sed -e "s/^$1\\.//" | sort -n | tail -n 1 || true)
+
+  local current_release_tag
+  local current_prerelease_tag
+  current_release_tag=$(git tag | grep -E "^$1\\.[0-9]+$" | grep -v 'rc' | sort -n | tail -n 1 || true)
+  current_prerelease_tag=$(git tag | grep -E "^$1\\.[0-9]+rc[0-9]+$" | grep "$current_release_tag"rc | sort -n | tail -n 1 || true)
+
+  active_release_branch=$(git branch -l -a | grep "release" || echo "False")
+
+  if [ "$current_release_suffix" == "" ]; then # the first tag in the series
+    local new_release_suffix=1
+  elif [[ $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ ^(hotfix-[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+|release-[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)$ ]]; then
+    current_release_suffix=$(git tag | grep -E "^$1\\.[0-9]+rc[0-9]+$" | sed -e "s/^$1\\.//" | sort -n | tail -n 1 | awk -F'rc' '{print $1}' | sort -n | tail -n 1)
+    local new_release_suffix=$current_release_suffix
+  elif [[ "${active_release_branch}" != "False" && $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ "qa" && $current_prerelease_tag ]] ; then
+    current_release_suffix=$(git tag | grep -E "^$1\\.[0-9]+rc[0-9]+$" | sed -e "s/^$1\\.//" | sort -n | tail -n 1 | awk -F'rc' '{print $1}' | sort -n | tail -n 1)
+    local new_release_suffix=$((current_release_suffix + 1))
+  elif [[ "${active_release_branch}" != "False" && $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ "qa" && $current_prerelease_tag == true ]] ; then
+    git tag "$current_release_tag"rc1
+    current_release_suffix=$(git tag | grep -E "^$1\\.[0-9]+rc[0-9]+$" | sed -e "s/^$1\\.//" | sort -n | tail -n 1 | awk -F'rc' '{print $1}' | sort -n | tail -n 1)
+    local new_release_suffix=$((current_release_suffix + 1))
+  else
+    local new_release_suffix=$((current_release_suffix + 1))
+  fi
+
+  if [[ $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ ^(qa|release-[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+|hotfix-[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)$ ]]; then
+    local current_prerelease_suffix
+    current_prerelease_suffix=$(git tag | grep -E "^${1}.${new_release_suffix}rc" | sed 's/.*rc//' | sort -n | tail -n 1 || true)
+
+    if [[ $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ ^(hotfix-[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+|release-[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)$ && "${active_release_branch}" != "False" ]]; then
+      existing_prerelease_suffix=$(git tag | grep -E "^${1}.${new_release_suffix}rc" | sed 's/.*rc//' | sort -n | tail -n 1 || true)
+      if [[ $existing_prerelease_suffix == "" ]]; then
+          echo "No existing rc tags for hotfix/release, continuing existing series"
+      else
+          current_prerelease_suffix=existing_prerelease_suffix
+      fi
+    fi
+
+    if [ "$current_prerelease_suffix" == "" ]; then # the first tag in the series
+      local new_prerelease_suffix=rc1
+    else
+      local new_prerelease_suffix=rc$((current_prerelease_suffix + 1))
+    fi
+  fi
+
+  new_tag="$1.$new_release_suffix${new_prerelease_suffix:-}"
+
+  echo "New tag is $1.$new_release_suffix${new_prerelease_suffix:-}"
+  if [ "${MAKE_TAGS:-}" != "" ]; then
+    git tag -a "$new_tag" -m "tag created from the kitchen by gitlabhelpers.tagmaker"
+    git push origin "$new_tag"
+  fi
+}
+
+# set up for pushes
+if [ "${MAKE_TAGS:-}" != "" ]; then
+  GITLAB_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /kitchen/secrets/_/gitlab_abacus_ai_api_token --output text --query Parameter.Value --with-decryption)
+  export GITLAB_TOKEN
+  git remote set-url origin "https://abacus-ai:${GITLAB_TOKEN}@gitlab.com/${CI_PROJECT_PATH}.git"
+fi
+
+if [ -f AUTO_SEMVER ]; then
+  source_mr=$(git show | grep 'See merge request' | sed 's/.*!//' || true)
+  if [ "${source_mr:-}" != "" ]; then
+    mr_labels=$(curl -sH "Authorization: Bearer ${GITLAB_TOKEN}" "${CI_API_V4_URL}/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}/merge_requests/${source_mr}" | jq -r .labels[])
+  fi
+
+  if [[ "${ENABLE_CONTINUOUS_DEPLOYMENT:-False}" == "True" ]]; then
+    echo "[INFO] Continuous Deployment Mode: Release branch bypass enabled. Proceeding with standard semver logic"
+    active_release_branch=""
+  else
+    active_release_branch=$(git branch --list "release-*" | grep -v 'release-.*rc' | sed -e 's/^[* ]*//' | head -n 1)
+  fi
+
+  if [[ $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "qa" && -n $active_release_branch && "${ENABLE_CONTINUOUS_DEPLOYMENT:-False}" != "True" ]]; then
+    # If it's QA and there's an active release branch (Continuous Delivery only), use its series
+    make_tag_from_prefix "$(echo "$active_release_branch" | sed -E 's/release-([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)/\1/')"
+  elif [[ $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH =~ ^(release-[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+|hotfix-[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)$ ]]; then
+    echo "[INFO] Release/hotfix branch detected"
+    make_tag_from_prefix "$(echo "$active_release_branch" | sed -E 's/release-([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)/\1/')"
+  else
+    current_major=$(git tag | sed -E 's/^([0-9]+).*/\1/' | sort -un | tail -n 1)
+
+    if [ "$current_major" == "" ]; then # first release, start at 0.0
+      make_tag_from_prefix 0.0
+    else
+      current_minor=$(git tag | grep "^$current_major." | sed -E 's/[0-9]+\.([0-9]+).*/\1/' | sort -un | tail -n 1)
+
+      if [[ ${mr_labels:-} =~ breaking ]]; then
+        make_tag_from_prefix $((current_major + 1)).0
+      elif [[ ${mr_labels:-} =~ new-feature ]]; then
+        make_tag_from_prefix "$current_major.$((current_minor + 1))"
+      else
+        make_tag_from_prefix "$current_major.$current_minor"
+      fi
+    fi
+  fi
+elif [ -f SEMVER ]; then
+  make_tag_from_prefix "$(cat SEMVER)"
+else
+  year=$(date +%y)
+  month=$(date +%m | sed 's/^0//')
+  tag_prefix="$year.$month"
+
+  make_tag_from_prefix "$tag_prefix"
+fi

approvals.title: DSP-7878: Streamline OTel -> Prometheus OTLP metrics path (minimal infra blast radius)
approvals.description:
## Intent
This MR hardens the OTel MVP to reliably deploy in our airgapped/CD environments while keeping scope tightly focused on the telemetry pipeline. Metrics are exported via OTLP to an existing in-cluster Prometheus endpoint; backend observability tooling itself is managed outside this repo.

## Why this update
- Align behavior with working component patterns (notably `databricks-mdm` and `ng-infrastructure`) for CI/CD and deploy scripts
- Remove flaky runtime dependencies (Terraform/Helm internet fetches in airflow deploys)
- Reduce infra blast radius by removing legacy in-repo backend components that are out of MVP scope
- Clarify architecture and ownership boundaries in docs

## What changed
### 1) OTel metrics path + collector hardening
- Kept OTLP-native metrics path to Prometheus (`/api/v1/otlp/v1/metrics`)
- Hardened collector normalization/cardinality controls and semconv alignment for Prometheus OTLP flow
- Updated demo/collector values and templates to match the minimal MVP telemetry path

### 2) Airgapped deploy reliability
- Terraform deploy scripts now use explicit pinned version behavior without runtime install (`tfenv use` if present; require `terraform` in PATH)
- Deploy/destroy scripts aligned closer to known-good component patterns
- Helm deploy path for airflow uses cached internal chart flow and desired-state rewrite for cached repos
- Replaced non-portable `rg` usage in deploy shell scripts with portable `grep`

### 3) CI/CD pipeline cleanup
- Added Helm cache template integration and `push:helm-cache` configuration for OTel desired state
- Removed custom `deploy-platform` rule divergence to match template behavior used by sibling components
- Added repo-owned `make-gitlab-tag` + `tag:version` override for consistent semver/RC tag creation
- Corrected semver/tagging setup for this MVP line (`0.1.x`) and cleaned malformed historical tag

### 4) Scope reduction to minimal MVP
- Removed legacy in-repo monitoring/backend stack artifacts (Grafana/Loki/Tempo/Prometheus chart assets and monitoring layer files)
- Kept this repo focused on OTel pipeline components (operator/collectors/demo path) rather than owning backend tooling

### 5) Documentation realignment
- Updated architecture/HLD/tech-spec docs to describe backend ownership boundary clearly
- Clarified MVP statement: metrics go to an existing in-cluster Prometheus instance, but Prometheus lifecycle/config outside this repo
- Added/updated minimal operating mode and healthcheck guidance

## Validation performed
- Full branch diff reviewed against `master`
- Shell/lint issues fixed for updated scripts (including SC2086 and command portability)
- Deploy flow and CI config aligned with existing proven patterns from sibling repos

## Blast radius / compatibility
- Preserves component-centric OTel deployment model
- Intentionally removes in-repo backend observability stack ownership
- Improves deployment reliability in airflow/airgapped execution contexts
